jobs.description: <p>We are currently recruiting for a Domestic Service Supervisor, to join an inclusive and inspiring school in Cambridge. You will join an extremely rewarding place to work with a dedicated, successful and welcoming team of both teaching professionals and pupils.<br /><br /><strong>Job Title:</strong> Domestic Service Supervisor<br /> <strong>Location:</strong> Cambridge<br /> <strong>Salary:</strong> £25901<br /> <strong>Hours: </strong>Monday- Friday, 06:00-13:30 <br /> Plus, Saturdays on a rota basis (07:00-10:00. Approx. 1 in 3 Saturdays)<br /> <strong>Availability:</strong> Immediate start date<br /><br /><strong><span style="text-decoration: underline;">General purpose and requirements</span></strong></p><ul><li>Laundry Management- Ensure all laundry paperwork is completed by domestics and returned laundry and dry cleaning is checked for shortages.</li><li>Collect Laundry invoices weekly and return to the Domestic Services Manager.</li><li>Maintaining Cleaning Standards- Check daily the standard of cleanliness in all areas in boarding-houses and classrooms, also completing two details checks per week.</li><li>To manage any cleaning problems, laundry discrepancies and people management issues, escalating where necessary.</li><li>Boarding Houses- Liaise with Matrons daily about any boarding house related problems including any maintenance jobs.</li><li>Check the quality of bedding replacements, i.e. duvets, mattresses etc. whilst also checking the cleanliness and quality of shower curtains, bathmats etc.</li><li>Health &amp; Safety- To check the correct usage of cleaning chemicals and ensure staff are wearing the correct PPE.</li><li>Ensure Domestics are following C.O.S.H.H &amp; Health and Safety Procedures.</li><li>To check the stock levels of cleaning material and to manage the stores (stock control/ purchasing)</li><li>This school is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and young people and expects all staff and volunteers to share this commitment.</li></ul><p><br /> A full list of duties and responsibilities will be sent over at the point of pre-screening.<br /><br /><strong><span style="text-decoration: underline;">Key Skills and Experience</span></strong></p><table><tbody><tr><td>Person Specification</td><td>Essential</td><td>Desirable</td></tr><tr><td>Experience</td><td>-Previous experience in a supervisory role within a housekeeping or cleaning department<br /> -Cleaning experience<br /> </td><td>-Cleaning experience within a school, hospital, or residential care setting<br /> </td></tr><tr><td>Skills</td><td>-Resilient and assertive<br /> -Able to supervise and motivate a team.<br /> -Able to work independently.<br /> -Able to work well within a large team.<br /> </td><td>-Ability to conduct appraisals and induction training.<br /> </td></tr><tr><td>Knowledge</td><td>-Interest in working within a school setting.<br /> -Good understanding of COSHH and how it applies in the school.<br /> -Good MS Office skills<br /> -Able to complete some administration tasks relating to checklists, invoices, and logging maintenance issues.<br /> </td><td>-Understanding of a boarding school or residential setting<br /> -Happy to undertake safeguarding training<br /> </td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /> If you are interested in applying, please submit your application.</p><p>If you receive suspicious outreach claiming to be from us, please contact us via the ManpowerGroup website.</p><img src="https://counter.adcourier.com/Tm9nYUouMzExMTUuMTM1OTVAbWFucG93ZXJ1a3Jpc2UuYXBsaXRyYWsuY29t.gif">

jobs.location: West End, London, West End, London, UK
jobs.description: <p><strong>Skilled Gardener Full Time or Part Time</strong><br /> Location: The Palace of Westminster Houses of Parliament / London<br /> Hourly Rate: £16.81 per hour<br /> Contract type: Temp-Perm<br /> Working hours: 3 days (24hrs) working within The Palace of Westminster Houses of Parliament.<br /> 2 days as a skilled gardener on a separate site, if full-time hours required- 40hrs)<br /><br /><strong>About the role<br /></strong><br />Our client currently provides horticultural &amp; landscaping services for the Palace of Westminster Estate. This is an exciting opportunity for a skilled &amp; experienced horticulturalist to get involved with the preparations around events like the State Opening of Parliament. There may at times be requests coming from multiple stakeholders (the Speaker's Office, the Ceremonial Works Office, or the Service Delivery Manager) all at once that will be reactive &amp; the candidate will be required to support the Head Gardener in managing these complex pressures.<br /><br /><strong>Responsibilities</strong><br /> * Regular site inspection<br /> * Clearance of litter, debris and detritus<br /> * Grass cutting, edging and cultural operations<br /> * Weeding, pruning and dead-heading of planted areas, including containers<br /> * Re-mulching / top-dressing planted areas and hedge bases<br /> * Hedge cutting and maintenance<br /> * Pleaching trees<br /> * Hard surface weed control<br /> * Hard surface cleansing, including sweeping and pressure washing<br /> * Maintenance of gravel areas<br /> * Leaf clearing - all areas<br /> * Green roof maintenance<br /> * Irrigation generally<br /> * Maintenance of internal plants, including trees at PCH<br /> <strong>In addition, you may be asked to support in the delivery of ad-hoc works, such as: </strong><br /> * Re-instatement of lawn areas following events<br /> * Emergency tree work<br /> * Replacement planting<br /> * Improvements to the green roof<br /> * Site improvement work<br /><br /><strong>Requirements </strong><br /> You will be required to possess the stipulated level of security vetting. We would ask that any successful applicant proactively engages with this vetting process as soon as an offer has been made, since it can take several weeks for the process to run its course. <br /> * N.B. A requirement of the vetting procedure is to supply HMRC evidence of the previous 3 years' employment.<br /> * Demonstratable high-horticultural skills and knowledge, backed up by a formal Horticultural qualification (equiv. L2 NVQ/RHS Hort).<br /> * Demonstratable experience on working with multiple stakeholders within the field of horticulture/landscaping. <br /> * Awareness of equality and diversity, health and safety and safeguarding.<br /> * Excellent people skills including team working, problem solving and the ability to be calm and decisive in challenging situations <br /> * A flexible approach to work<br /> * Harness Training would be desirable.<br /><br /><strong>Why join us?</strong><br /><br />As the UK's leading grounds maintenance and landscape creation provider, no two projects are ever the same - and neither are our people. That's why we're committed to creating a diverse and inclusive culture where everyone can thrive.<br /><br /> We're proud to have been recognised as Employer of the Year and Sustainable Company of the Year at this year's Landscaper Awards, reflecting our commitment to supporting our people while delivering a more sustainable future.<br /><br /><strong>What we offer</strong><br /><br />* Career development: We offer career advancement and development opportunities through our bespoke apprenticeship programmes, our extensive in-house training academy, and coaching and mentorship.<br /> * Collaborative team environment: Join a diverse and inclusive workplace where you can collaborate with passionate and talented individuals.<br /> * Meaningful impact: Contribute to projects that make a real difference in the community and environment.<br /> * Competitive benefits package: We recognise and reward your hard work with our wide-ranging benefits package designed to prioritise your wellbeing, support life choices, and help in both good and challenging times.<br /><br /><strong>Benefits</strong><br /><br />* Annual leave: from 21 days plus bank holidays.<br /> * Family-friendly policies: Enhanced maternity and shared parental leave.<br /> * Thrive Wellbeing App support: Support for personal and professional challenges.<br /> * Voluntary benefits: Discounts on retail, holidays, gym memberships, and more.<br /> * Financial wellbeing support: Resources to manage your finances.<br /> * Recognition schemes: Colleague of the month and annual awards.<br /> * Volunteering policy: Two days per year to support a cause of your choice.<br /> * Mental health support: Comprehensive resources and support.<br /><br /><strong>About our client</strong><br /><br />We are Europe's largest provider of grounds maintenance services and landscape creation, in the UK alone we're a community of over 3,000 passionate colleagues shaping the future of the green industry.<br /><br />We understand what it means to grow. Our history dates back to 1919, and today, with a turnover of £250 million, we're more than just a company - we're a force for positive change. We offer a range of services to create, maintain, and manage landscapes across the UK.<br /><br /><strong>Grow with us, and together we'll create a greener future for all.</strong></p><p>If you receive suspicious outreach claiming to be from us, please contact us via the ManpowerGroup website.</p><img src="https://counter.adcourier.com/Qy5DYW1wYmVsbC40MjcwOS4xMzU5NUBtYW5wb3dlcnVrcmlzZS5hcGxpdHJhay5jb20.gif">

jobs.description: <p><strong>Cost Controlling Analyst</strong><br /> <strong>Department:</strong> Controlling<br /> <strong>Location:</strong> Milton Keynes<br /> <strong>Salary:</strong> £34,088.60</p><p><strong>Contract:</strong> Temp, until end of 2026. <br /><br />We are looking for a <strong>Cost Controlling Analyst</strong> to join our team in Milton Keynes. This role plays a key part in driving financial performance through effective budget management, cost control, forecasting and insightful business partnering across the organisation.<br /> You will support senior stakeholders by delivering high-quality financial analysis, improving cost efficiency, and providing actionable insights that support informed decision-making at all levels of the business.<br /> You will provide recommended action plans to budget holders and resource managers, supporting mitigation of financial risks and issues and influencing decision-making across the business.<br /><br /><strong>Key Responsibilities</strong></p><ul><li>Support the <strong>General Manager - Change Controlling</strong> and the wider Controlling function in accurate forecasting, budget management and benefit delivery</li><li>Monitor and analyse cost centre budgets against the latest HQ forecast, identifying variances and recommending corrective actions</li><li>Maintain a strong understanding of overhead and capital expenditure, holding regular review meetings with cost centre managers</li><li>Drive and track <strong>cost optimisation initiatives</strong> against agreed targets</li><li>Develop and maintain the <strong>monthly total company overheads report</strong> using SAP data, ensuring material variances are clearly explained</li><li>Manage month-end processes to strict deadlines, providing insightful commentary on movements</li><li>Process month-end and year-end journals in SAP, ensuring costs are accurately recorded by project, cost centre, account and period</li><li>Identify and implement process efficiencies, particularly around month-end close and automation</li><li>Prepare structured presentations and insights to support effective storytelling and decision-making</li><li>Support ad-hoc finance projects, analysis, investigations and IT/Finance system testing as required</li><li>Build strong working relationships across Finance, Controlling, HQ and the wider business to share best practice</li></ul><p><br /><br /><strong>Skills, Experience &amp; Qualifications</strong><br /> <strong>Essential:</strong></p><ul><li>Proven experience in finance, controlling or cost management roles</li><li>Strong financial analysis, reporting and forecasting experience</li><li>Advanced MS Excel skills</li><li>Excellent attention to detail and strong analytical capability</li><li>Ability to manage workload, prioritise effectively and work under pressure</li><li>Confident communicator, able to challenge and influence stakeholders at all levels</li></ul><p><strong>Desirable:</strong></p><ul><li>Experience with SAP</li><li>Data visualisation tools (e.g. Power BI, Tableau)</li><li>Working towards a professional qualification (CIMA, ACCA, ACA) or equivalent experience</li></ul><p><br /> <strong>About our client:</strong><br /> A leading provider of financial services and mobility solutions, supporting vehicle finance, leasing and related financial products. Our focus is on delivering strong financial performance, innovation and high-quality service within a fast-paced, commercial environment.<br /> Apply now and a member of our team will be in touch.</p><p>If you receive suspicious outreach claiming to be from us, please contact us via the ManpowerGroup website.</p><img src="https://counter.adcourier.com/ZS5tY2tlZS4wNDUxOC4xMzU5NUBtYW5wb3dlcnVrcmlzZS5hcGxpdHJhay5jb20.gif">

jobs.description: <p>Brook Street are currently working with a leading machinery manufacturer business who are looking to recruit an organised and proactive Service Coordinator to join their team in Bradford on a full-time permanent basis.<br /><br /><strong>Role Purpose</strong><br /><br />The Service Coordinator will be responsible for efficiently managing the day-to-day operations of a team of Field Service Engineers, ensuring that customer requirements and service level agreements are consistently met or exceeded. You will deliver effective planning and coordination of engineering activities to support operational efficiency and customer satisfaction.<br /> </p><ul><li>Monday to Friday</li><li>Full time permanent position</li><li><strong>Salary on offer: £28,000- £30,000</strong></li><li>Hours: 8:00- 5:00pm</li><li>Location: Bradford</li><li>Annual bonus scheme</li><li>Private healthcare scheme</li><li>33 days holiday (including bank holidays)</li><li>Ongoing technical and product training</li><li>Laptop provided</li><li>Employee Assistance Programme</li><li>Free on-site parking</li></ul><p><br /> <strong>Key Responsibilities</strong></p><ul><li>Act as the first point of contact for customers, handling incoming calls and emails relating to service requests and breakdowns.</li><li>Plan, schedule and process routine maintenance and emergency service calls.</li><li>Manage service administration, including processing and invoicing of completed work.</li><li>Develop and maintain detailed service plans and schedules.</li><li>Communicate daily with a small team of Field Service Engineers to coordinate workloads and priorities.</li><li>Prepare and issue quotations for additional works as required.</li><li>Identify and escalate business opportunities to relevant internal departments.</li><li>Maintain accurate and up-to-date customer records using the CRM system.</li><li>Collaborate with internal teams to optimise the efficiency and utilisation of engineering resources.</li><li>Manage stock control requirements for the engineering team.</li><li>Provide proactive updates and communication to customers regarding service progress.</li></ul><p><br /> <strong>What we are looking for:</strong></p><ul><li>Strong organisational and forward-planning abilities</li><li>Excellent communication and interpersonal skills</li><li>Good level of IT literacy, including experience with CRM systems</li><li>Ability to work collaboratively within a team environment</li><li>Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills</li></ul><p><br /> If you are interested in this opportunity and meet the requirements above, apply today through Brook Street Recruitment Agency, send across your CV or call 0113 2438871. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for further discussion.<br /><br /></p><p>If you receive suspicious outreach claiming to be from us, please contact us via the ManpowerGroup website.</p><img src="https://counter.adcourier.com/QXJzYWxhbi5Nb2hhbW1lZC42NjgyNy4xMzU5NUBtYW5wb3dlcnVrcmlzZS5hcGxpdHJhay5jb20.gif">

Lifting, meeting the eye test requirements of the role</li></ul><p>If you receive suspicious outreach claiming to be from us, please contact us via the ManpowerGroup website.</p><img src="https://counter.adcourier.com/UmFobWFuUy40NjQwNy4xMzU5NUBtYW5wb3dlcnVrcmlzZS5hcGxpdHJhay5jb20.gif">

<p><strong>Park &amp; Play Infrastructure Supervisor</strong><br /> Location: Bromley Borough, reporting to High Elms Country Park<br /> Salary: £30,000<br /> Contract Type: Permanent, Full-time<br /> Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 7am to 3:30pm<br /><br /><strong>About the role</strong><br /><br />The Parks &amp; Play Infrastructure Supervisor plays a key operational and supervisory role within the Parks Development Team. The postholder will lead a small reactive repairs team responsible for addressing health &amp; safety (H&amp;S) and security priorities across parks and open spaces.<br /><br />This is a hands-on supervisory role, requiring both leadership and active participation in repair and maintenance works, particularly relating to play infrastructure, street furniture, and small-scale construction.<br /><br /><strong>Key Responsibilities</strong><br /><br />* Team Leadership &amp; Supervision: managing a small reactive response repairs team, through providing daily direction and ensuring work is completed safely, efficiently and to a high standard, and through training of staff.<br /> * Health &amp; Safety Compliance: ensure Health and Safety is prioritised for both staff and the public at all times, through Risk Assessments and Method Statements (RAMS), COSHH assessments, PPE checks and audits.<br /> * Operations &amp; Reactive Maintenance: respond to priorities raised through public reporting system, orchestrating and conducting regular site inspections across parks, supervising and undertaking maintenance &amp; repairs of park infrastructure and play equipment, and ensuring jobs are delivered using safe, cost-effective and efficient methods.<br /> * Project Co-ordination &amp; Stakeholder Management: liaise with subcontractors to obtain quotes, assist in co-ordination of and updating on small-scale projects, and communicate effectively with Council Officers, Friends Groups and contractors through in-person, email and phone.<br /> * Administration &amp; Compliance: maintain accurate records including equipment logs, stock, timesheets, absences, and HAV records, ensuring all documentation (RAMS, reports, audits) is up to date, and working closely with the Administration Team and Park Engagement Co-ordinators.<br /><br /><strong>What We're Looking For</strong><br /><br /><strong>Essential Skills &amp; Experience</strong><br /><br />* Full UK Driving Licence.<br /> * Proven experience supervising multi-disciplinary teams across concurrent projects.<br /> * Experience quoting soft and hard landscaping works up to circa £15,000.<br /> * Strong practical knowledge of street furniture repairs and small-scale construction works.<br /> * Ability to identify issues and implement effective solutions.<br /> * Good IT skills (reporting systems, email, basic software).<br /><br /><strong>Desirable</strong><br /><br />* Playground inspection qualification (e.g. RPII or equivalent).<br /> * Experience in playground surfacing repairs.<br /> * Previous experience working in parks or public open spaces.<br /> * Experience managing small budgets.<br /> * Construction qualification or accreditation.<br /><br /><strong>Why join us?</strong><br /><br />As the UK's leading grounds maintenance and landscape creation provider, no two projects are ever the same - and neither are our people. That's why we're committed to nurturing a diverse and inclusive culture where everyone can thrive.<br /><br /><strong>What we offer</strong><br /><br />* We offer career advancement and development opportunities through our bespoke apprenticeship programmes, our extensive in-house training academy, and coaching and mentorship.<br /> * Join a diverse and inclusive workplace where you can collaborate with passionate and talented individuals.<br /> * Contribute to projects that make a real difference in the community and environment.<br /> * We recognise and reward your hard work with our wide-ranging benefits package designed to prioritise your wellbeing, support life choices, and help in both good and challenging times.<br /><br /><strong>Benefits</strong><br /><br />* 21 days holiday plus bank holidays.<br /> * Enhanced maternity and shared parental leave.<br /> * Support for personal and professional challenges.<br /> * Discounts on retail, holidays, gym memberships, and more.<br /> * Secure your future - competitive pension scheme and resources to manage your finances.<br /> * Colleague of the month and annual awards.<br /> * Two days per year to support a cause of your choice.<br /> * Comprehensive resources and support.<br /><br /><strong>About our client</strong><br /><br />We are Europe's largest provider of grounds maintenance services and landscape creation, in the UK alone we're a community of over 3,000 passionate colleagues shaping the future of the green industry.<br /><br />We understand what it means to grow. Our history dates back to 1919, and today, with a turnover of £250 million, we're more than just a company - we're a force for positive change. We offer a range of services to create, maintain, and manage landscapes across the UK.<br /><br />Grow with us, and together we'll create a greener future for all.</p><p>If you receive suspicious outreach claiming to be from us, please contact us via the ManpowerGroup website.</p><img src="https://counter.adcourier.com/Qy5DYW1wYmVsbC42MzU1Ny4xMzU5NUBtYW5wb3dlcnVrcmlzZS5hcGxpdHJhay5jb20.gif">

jobs.description: <p><strong>Production Operative - Oral Care Compounding </strong><br /><br />Monday to Friday <br /> 2 shifts are 06.00 - 14.00 and 12.00 - 20.00 - weekly rotation <br /><br />Under limited supervision of Shift Leader/Production Operator be responsible for operating manufacturing machines to produce materials of varying complexity in accordance with targets and following established procedures.<br /><br /><strong>Core Responsibilities</strong><br /> </p><ul><li>Operate designated machines and production equipment such as blenders, sieves, spray dryer systems, batching systems, multipurpose reactors, spinning cone column, bulk tanks, automated dosing robots, packaging systems, ovens, mills, distillation columns (stills), encapsulation systems. Common tasks involve several of the following activities: blending, mixing, dispensing, packaging, heating, cooking, adding enzymes, compounding, filtering.</li><li>Select intermediates or raw materials, move and load it into production machines.</li><li>Understand bill of materials and/or formula specifications, so the proper quantities of materials are added in the proper sequence.</li><li>Manufacture product in a safe and timely manner and according to predefined process instructions to meet specific quality standards.</li><li>Accurately read control instruments, prepare neat &amp; accurate records for all operations as required.</li><li>Able to troubleshoot basic problems independently and provide recommendations for correction. Able to adjust colouring to the compounds.</li><li>Perform in-process quality testing to ensure customer specifications are met. Identify deviations and document/communicate them in compliance with Standard Operating Procedures.</li><li>Prepare product packaging for next step in production or for final shipment to customer according to department specifications.</li><li>Maintain neat, orderly and clean shop floor and workplace at all times and complete all sanitation and housekeeping duties as required.</li><li>Work in accordance with all relevant requirements including, but not limited to GMP, AIB, OSHA, HACCP, FDA, USDA, ISO'S and internal safety guidelines.</li></ul><p><br /><br /><strong>Professional Experience</strong><br /> </p><ul><li>Experience in flavour, ingredient, food, fragrance or chemical manufacturing preferred</li><li>Minimum 1 year forklift operation and/or in-house fork truck certification is required preferred</li></ul><ul><li>SAP bar coding/scanning experience</li><li>Basic maths, reading and recordkeeping skills.</li><li>Basic PC skills, including competency in MS Office products.</li><li>Experience in using RF scanner and labelling.</li><li>Ingredient knowledge around colour, odour, order of addition and other pertinent quality factors.</li></ul><p> </p><p>If you receive suspicious outreach claiming to be from us, please contact us via the ManpowerGroup website.</p><img src="https://counter.adcourier.com/bGF2aW5pYS5pb3JnYS41ODQwNy4xMzU5NUBtYW5wb3dlcnVrcmlzZS5hcGxpdHJhay5jb20.gif">
